conjecture_title: Question (rainbow hole with $s$ consecutive rainbow vertices)
paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. IX. Rainbow paths
summary: The question asks whether, for all fixed $s$ and $\kappa$, every graph $G$ with $\omega(G)\leq\kappa$ and sufficiently large $\chi(G)$ must contain, in every colouring, a hole with some $s$ consecutive rainbow vertices. The source paper (1702.01094) shows that the stronger property — a fully rainbow hole, or even one in which every three consecutive vertices are rainbow — fails via a shift-graph construction, leaving the weaker $s$-consecutive variant open. A 2026 preprint (arXiv:2601.00602) improves quantitative bounds on induced rainbow paths in triangle-free graphs but does not address holes. No resolution of the hole question was found in the indexed literature.

since_posted.title: Towards a conjecture on long induced rainbow paths in Δ-free graphs
since_posted.claim: Improves quantitative bounds on induced rainbow paths (not holes) in triangle-free graphs beyond Scott–Seymour's earlier bound, but does not address the rainbow-hole question.

search_queries: rainbow hole consecutive vertices chromatic number Scott Seymour induced subgraphs
rainbow path hole graph coloring chi-bounded clique-free 1702.01094
Scott Seymour "induced subgraphs large chromatic number" rainbow hole 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
rainbow hole "consecutive rainbow" vertices graph chromatic number 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The shift graph of triples (coloured by middle elements) rules out a fully rainbow hole and a hole in which every three consecutive vertices are rainbow, but the weaker question of $s$ consecutive rainbow vertices in a hole is open. The paper arXiv:2601.00602 is the closest follow-up found; it handles paths only. No paper resolving the hole variant was found after five web calls.
